slides.title: HTTP Header Fields for Proxying DNS SVCB Information
ECN and DSCP support for HTTPS's Connect-UDP

chat.author: Kazuho Oku
chat.text: +1 to Tommy / thank you to the authors for addressing the issues

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: +1 to Zahed, it would be good to write down where we expect future maintenance to happen

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: Mic line consistently made up of former transport ADs

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: yes we love charters!

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: But yeah the email that closes the group will need to say that future MASQUE-related work should go to HTTPBIS

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: That doesn't need to live in the charter though

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: not in this charter but in the tombstone

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: Why is 65535 not a valid length?

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: but I never got why we are rushing to recharter..can't we decide on the masque arch and qlog work .. finish the conpression and DSCP work and then close?

chat.author: Mike Bishop
chat.text: The arch doc isn't currently in charter.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: this current update doesnot help taking the arch draft eirther.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: it should then say we want to work on the architecture, not may adopt.

chat.author: Marten Seemann
chat.text:
it does: "It may also adopt an
informational document describing the MASQUE architecture"

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: I don't think so.. what does may mean here if you want to really work on the arch doc

chat.author: Marten Seemann
chat.text: it means that the WG is allowed to adopt the document, but actual adoption will depend on the outcome of an adoption call

chat.author: Mike Bishop
chat.text: Does "rushing" = discussing for >1 year? :wink:

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: My understanding is that we want to work on arch that is why we want to recharter..

chat.author: Dennis Jackson
chat.text: I don't think a charter can force adoption of a document. Nor is there any need for it to.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: 1 year in IETF calendar might be rushing, yes :-)

chat.author: Dennis Jackson
chat.text: We are trying to spend the minimum WG time and energy on process stuff like the recharter, vs getting drafts out the door.

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: You can write that the working group will work on arch but then you may not adopt anything bcos of disagreement and don't publish anything

chat.author: Dennis Jackson
chat.text: That is possible.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: Is there any reason that the 4 codepoints for each DSCP need to be discontiuous?  The examples show simple sequential assignment, could that be replaced by a single 1:1 mapping?

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: Because you can have (48, 2, 4, 6, 8) and (48, 4, 2, 8, 6) and (48, 20, 22, 60, 48), which seems a bit weird.

chat.author: Marten Seemann
chat.text: @mt Want to ask this at the mic?

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Martin this is to fulfill the Context ID assignment rules where even are assigned by one side and odds by the other

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: The example are: ECN-DSCP-Context-ID =(48,8,10,12,14), (0,0,2,4,6,)

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: So it is actually continous assignements of CID for each DSCP assignments.

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text:
I think the big argument for doing it this way is that the vast majority of hostnames still don't have SVCB/HTTPS RRs.
But this does put all of the logic into the client otherwise, and just gets it what it needs to do so.

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text: Note that the "cancel" is mostly useful for HTTP/1.1 proxies and even then if we consider it safe to reuse them after the cancel.

chat.author: Lucas Pardue
chat.text: :protocol: connect-lambda

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: I'm thinking we might want to go a step further and define "connect-http" with well defined rules about how the proxy queries HTTPS and then establishes TLS or QUIC (the client would send a ClientHello and an INITIAL etc)

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text:
Martin Thomson said:

Is there any reason that the 4 codepoints for each DSCP need to be discontiuous?  The examples show simple sequential assignment, could that be replaced by a single 1:1 mapping?

you mean we could say this is the DSCP and the first context ID and automatically assign also the next three? that saves some bytes in the assign capsule but do we need to save those bytes? Otherwise I just think it doesn't matter.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text:
This business of getting SVCB parameters is not coming at the right time.  In order to use Happy Eyeballs, you need that information before selecting an address to test.  So you really do need to make a DoH request before initiating the connection attempt.
This sort of thing comes later, when you are looking to optimize that entire process, maybe by prewarming the client-proxy hop by skipping various steps (optimistically).

chat.author: Mike Bishop
chat.text: If Proxy is h1, the Optimistic doc says you should kill the connection if you don't accept the CONNECT, no?

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text:
Mirja Kühlewind said:

Martin Thomson said:

Is there any reason that the 4 codepoints for each DSCP need to be discontiuous?  The examples show simple sequential assignment, could that be replaced by a single 1:1 mapping?

you mean we could say this is the DSCP and the first context ID and automatically assign also the next three? that saves some bytes in the assign capsule but do we need to save those bytes? Otherwise I just think it doesn't matter.

Yes.  Not just that, but you are guaranteed to get a contiguous block, rather than a fragmented one.  That makes implementation simpler and less error prone.  Fewer corner cases to test out and so forth.

chat.author: Marten Seemann
chat.text: Are we doing all this complexity just to save one RTT? And a very short RTT in most cases, since MASQUE proxies are often located at a CDN edge?

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text:
Mike Bishop said:

If Proxy is h1, the Optimistic doc says you should kill the connection if you don't accept the CONNECT, no?

You might want to avoid certain optimizations in that case .

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text:
If Proxy is h1, the Optimistic doc says you should kill the connection if you don't accept the CONNECT, no?

This is a potential alternative, but full of sharp edges.

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text:
Martin Thomson said:

Mirja Kühlewind said:

Martin Thomson said:

Is there any reason that the 4 codepoints for each DSCP need to be discontiuous?  The examples show simple sequential assignment, could that be replaced by a single 1:1 mapping?

you mean we could say this is the DSCP and the first context ID and automatically assign also the next three? that saves some bytes in the assign capsule but do we need to save those bytes? Otherwise I just think it doesn't matter.

Yes.  Not just that, but you are guaranteed to get a contiguous block, rather than a fragmented one.  That makes implementation simpler and less error prone.  Fewer corner cases to test out and so forth.

maybe. open an issue? thx!

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: @Mirja Kühlewind could you give me a pointer to the repo?

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: https://github.com/gloinul/masque-ecn ?

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: https://github.com/gloinul/masque-ecn

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: yes, we should probably move this to the wg repo

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: @Dennis Jackson I think that the previous draft belongs HERE, not DNSOP.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: I fear that DNSOP will think that this is totally fine, when the harm being done is to HTTP, on the assumption that the HTTP people think it is fine.

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: at least DNSOP doesn't seem the right place

chat.author: Lucas Pardue
chat.text: I ain't going dnsop

chat.author: Valentin Goșu
chat.text: httpbis?

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text: In the Happy Eyeballs world, this is optimizing some of the common happy paths (eg, when there is no SVCB/HTTPS RR, and when the {TargetName,proto,port} matches what you already told the proxy to connect to.

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text: I think I agree that here or httpbis (or happy?) is better than dnsop.

chat.author: Dennis Jackson
chat.text: Apologies for cutting short the discussion on the dnsop draft. If folks want to discuss the appropriate venue further, we can spend some minutes after Yaroslav's presentation.

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: No need to apologize.  You ran out of time.  And it's not a crisp thing.

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: Happy to chat more about the dns draft if we're at the end of the agenda

chat.author: Dennis Jackson
chat.text: We are indeed at the end of the agenda.

chat.author: Eric Kinnear
chat.text: I don't think that's actually CONNECT-HTTP, but I like the inclusion of the ClientInitial and ClientHello both

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: Maybe a MASQUE-HTTPBIS-HAPPY-DNSOP joint meeting on Monday morning?

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: Masqued Eyeballs?

chat.author: Lucas Pardue
chat.text: Mhh

chat.author: Zaheduzzaman Sarker
chat.text: happy masque.. jokes apart.. shouldn't happy wg should think about the tunnel cases?
